exampleSentences: Anh cần cái này ngay bây giờ.
Em mua cái đó cho mẹ.
Chị thấy cái mới ở cửa hàng.
notes_md:
**cái** usually means “thing,” but it’s mainly a **classifier** for objects in Vietnamese. You use it before many inanimate nouns: objects, tools, furniture, general items.


It’s the most common, “default” classifier when you’re not sure which one to use. It can also add a slightly emotional or emphatic tone in some phrases (like when pointing out or criticizing something).

Strong memory tip: think of **cái** as the go-to word for physical “stuff” you can point at.

definitions.es: Un clasificador utilizado para objetos, a menudo indicando un artículo específico.
definitions.en: A classifier used for objects, often indicating a specific item.
definitions.vi: Một từ phân loại dùng cho đồ vật, thường chỉ một món đồ cụ thể.

notes:
cái usually means “thing,” but it’s mainly a classifier for objects in Vietnamese. You use it before many inanimate nouns: objects, tools, furniture, general items.

It’s the most common, “default” classifier when you’re not sure which one to use. It can also add a slightly emotional or emphatic tone in some phrases (like when pointing out or criticizing something).

Strong memory tip: think of cái as the go-to word for physical “stuff” you can point at.
sentenceBreakdowns: Anh (he) + cần (need) + cái (thing) + này (this) + ngay bây giờ (right now)
Em (I) + mua (buy) + cái (thing) + đó (that) + cho (for) + mẹ (mom)
Chị (she) + thấy (see) + cái (thing) + mới (new) + ở (at) + cửa hàng (store)
exampleSentencesTranslated: He needs this thing right now.
I buy that thing for mom.
She sees the new thing at the store.
